entry.title: Re: Workflow hierarchy approval setup for condition contract
entry.content:
Hi @arindamsur 

You can achieve this in the Manage Workflows for Condition Contracts app when using Flexible workflow.

You have to create multiple steps in the Workflow Steps section of this app.

Step1: New Decision step for EmployeeA

Step2: Decision step for EmployeeB

Step3: Release of Condition Contract step for EmployeeC.

I would recommend you to try with Settlement Document approval using Manage workflow for Settlement documents first. This will give you an understanding on how to use these apps.

Although this is not a complete explanation, I hope it proves helpful to you in some way.

Regards,

Anjhi

entry.title: RAP Odata V4 APIs from S4HANA public cloud not visible in BAS
entry.content:
I have created a RAP Application in S4HANA Public cloud using ADT. I have expodes this application as an OData V4 UI service. The preview of the application works fine.

I created a BTP destination for the S4HANA Public Cloud dev system (client 080).
The destination properties have WebIDEEnabled = true and WebIDEUsage = odata_abap,dev_abap,odata_gen

In SAP Business Application Studio, when I create a Fiori List Report using Template and choose the S4HANA public cloud destination, I am unable to find my Odata V4 service.I can see only OData V2 services.

I could not find much hints in SAP community blogs except that in few blogs they have mentioned settings w.r.t /IWFND/CONFIG and other V4 service group nodes but then all of these are On Premise related.

Is there a restriction on the availability of OData V4 Service in BAS? Or am I missing any configuration.

entry.title: EDI vs. SAP CPI: Choosing the Best Solution for Supplier Order Confirmations
entry.content:
In today’s fast-moving business world, keeping your supply chain running smoothly is more important than ever. A key part of that is automating supplier order confirmations. However, businesses often ask: Do we need to use EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) to automate these confirmations, or can we convert vendor email confirmations into SAP order confirmations using SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI)?

In this blog, we’re going to break down both options, compare their benefits and challenges, and help you figure out which solution will work best for your business.

1. Introduction: Activating Supplier Order Confirmations in SAP

When you place an order with a supplier, you expect a confirmation to make sure everything is correct and the order is being processed. Traditionally, these confirmations are sent by email, fax, or phone, which can slow things down and lead to mistakes.

To make things easier and faster, companies use SAP to automate and track order confirmations. The big question is: Should you use EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) or SAP CPI (Cloud Platform Integration) to handle the data between your system and your suppliers?  we’ll compare both options and help you choose the best one for your business.

 

2. How EDI Automates Supplier Order Confirmations

EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) is a way for businesses to send documents, like order confirmations, electronically in a standard format. When both you and your supplier use EDI, the process of exchanging order confirmations becomes automatic and much more efficient.

How it works:

  • The supplier sends the order confirmation through EDI, using a format like IDoc, EDIFACT, or X12.
  • Your SAP system gets the EDI message, processes it, and automatically creates an order confirmation in SAP.

Advantages:

  • Fully Automated: Once set up, everything happens automatically, with little to no manual work.
  • Standardized: EDI uses a common format that both systems understand, making it easy to exchange data.
  • Reliable: Since it’s automated, there’s less room for human error, making the process more accurate.

Considerations:

  • Requires Both Systems to Support EDI: Both you and your supplier need to have EDI set up.
  • Setup and Maintenance Costs: EDI can be expensive to set up and maintain, as it requires technical configuration and ongoing support.

In short, EDI is a great way to automate order confirmations, but it does require some upfront investment from both sides to set it up. Once it's running, it works smoothly and reliably.

 

3. Automating Order Confirmations Without EDI: SAP CPI for Email-Based Confirmations

Not every supplier uses EDI. Some prefer to send order confirmations by email, often in formats like PDF, Excel, or Word. This is where SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI) comes in. It lets you automatically process email-based order confirmations into your SAP system—no need for EDI.

How it works:

  • The supplier sends an order confirmation via email (usually as a PDF, Excel, or Word file).
  • SAP CPI reads the email and pulls out the important details like order number, quantities, and delivery info.
  • It then converts this data into a format SAP can use (like IDoc or BAPI) and updates your SAP system with the order confirmation.

Advantages:

  • No EDI Needed: If your supplier doesn’t have EDI, SAP CPI lets you process their email confirmations without extra tech.
  • Flexible: SAP CPI can handle different email formats (PDF, Word, Excel) and turn them into usable data for SAP.
  • Cost-Effective for Small Suppliers: Small suppliers who can’t invest in EDI can still send email confirmations, and you can automate the process using SAP CPI.

Considerations:

  • Complex Email Formats: Parsing unstructured data from PDFs or free-text emails can be tricky and might need a custom setup in SAP CPI.
  • Ongoing Maintenance: If your supplier changes their email format, you may need to adjust the parsing rules in SAP CPI to keep everything working smoothly.

In short, SAP CPI is a great option for automating email-based order confirmations when your supplier doesn’t use EDI. It’s easy to set up and saves time, but it does need a bit of maintenance to handle different email formats.

 

4. Which Solution Is Right for You? EDI vs. SAP CPI

  • EDI is the best choice if both you and your supplier support it. It’s the most efficient, automated, and standardized way to manage order confirmations.
  • SAP CPI is a great option if your supplier doesn’t use EDI or prefers to send order confirmations by email. It’s flexible and can handle different email formats, but it might need more setup and ongoing maintenance.

5. Conclusion: Simplifying Supplier Order Confirmations

If your supplier already uses EDI, sticking with it is probably the best choice. It’s fast, reliable, and fully automated, making it easier to keep everything in sync.

If your supplier prefers to send email confirmations, SAP CPI can handle that too. While it may require some setup and maintenance, it allows you to automate the processing of emails into your SAP system, even if EDI isn’t an option.

Do You Need EDI for Both Systems?

No, you don’t need EDI if you’re using SAP CPI to process email-based confirmations. However, if both parties support EDI, it’s usually the more efficient and standardized choice.

By understanding the benefits and limitations of both options, you can choose the right solution to streamline your order confirmation process. Whether you use EDI or SAP CPI, automating this process will save time, reduce errors, and make your supply chain more efficient.

Hope this blog is helpful! Feel free to reach out if you have any questions or need help setting up either solution!

entry.title: Re: ABAP RAP MAP_MESSAGES is not triggerred when foreign...
entry.content: Hello Juwin, Thank you for the answer. This makes sense. Hopefully SAP has a solution for this in the near future releases as this is definitely a good feature to have.

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Porto 2025: Exploring the Future of SAP Innovation
entry.content:
SAP Inside Track Porto 2025: Exploring the Future of SAP Innovation

The SAP Inside Track Porto 2025 promises to be a landmark event in the SAP community, bringing together experts, thought leaders, and SAP enthusiasts from all over the globe to explore the cutting-edge innovations that are reshaping the future of enterprise technology. Hosted at the The Lodge Hotel Porto on September 27th, 2025, the event will dive into some of the most transformative trends in SAP technology today, including the migration to S/4HANA, advancements in Generative AI (Gen AI), the rise of Instant Payments, and the pursuit of Green Ledger solutions for sustainable business practices.

In this article, we’ll give you a sneak peek into the key topics set to be discussed, why they matter for businesses of all sizes, and how SAP is playing a central role in the evolution of enterprise technology.

  • The SAP Revolution: From S/4HANA to the Future

One of the most pressing topics for businesses worldwide is the migration to SAP S/4HANA. As SAP continues its push toward a cloud-first future, migrating from legacy systems to S/4HANA is no longer just a technical upgrade; it’s a strategic move that can unlock new capabilities, streamline operations, and improve business outcomes.

At SAP Inside Track Porto 2025, experts will share insights into the S/4HANA migration journey, focusing on the different approaches organizations can take. The discussion will address the Greenfield vs. Brownfield strategies, helping businesses choose the best path based on their current systems and goals. Presenters will also explore the importance of business process transformation during the migration, as well as the critical role that the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) plays in connecting systems, driving innovation, and ensuring a smooth transition to the cloud.

As part of the Intelligent Enterprise framework, SAP S/4HANA empowers businesses to automate processes, gain real-time insights, and improve collaboration. Whether you're just starting your migration journey or looking to optimize an existing S/4HANA system, SAP Inside Track Porto 2025 will provide the tools, knowledge, and strategies needed to make the most of this powerful platform.

  • Generative AI: Unlocking New Possibilities

The rise of Generative AI (Gen AI) is one of the most exciting advancements in the tech world today, and it’s quickly making its way into SAP solutions. At SAP Inside Track Porto 2025, Gen AI will take center stage as a key area of discussion. With applications ranging from enhanced decision-making to automated workflows, Generative AI is transforming how businesses use data to drive innovation and improve performance.

SAP is already incorporating AI-driven solutions into its S/4HANA and SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) offerings. SAP Business AI and SAP Copilot are just a few examples of how AI is being embedded within SAP applications to enhance user experiences, optimize operations, and deliver personalized insights. Whether it’s automating manual tasks, predicting market trends, or creating advanced analytics models, Gen AI can empower organizations to make smarter, faster decisions.

At the event, attendees will gain hands-on experience with SAP AI Core and AI Foundation, SAP’s tools designed to help businesses deploy and scale AI models across various applications. The conversations around AI in the enterprise will focus on how SAP is helping organizations leverage AI to drive growth, efficiency, and competitive advantage in an increasingly automated world.

  • Instant Payments: A New Era of Financial Transactions

As businesses and consumers alike demand faster and more secure payment methods, Instant Payments are becoming a critical theme for SAP and the broader financial ecosystem. SAP's instant payment solutions, built on advanced cloud technologies, are revolutionizing how companies handle transactions, particularly in industries like retail, banking, and manufacturing.

During SAP Inside Track Porto 2025, sessions will focus on how SAP is helping businesses adopt and integrate real-time payment capabilities into their financial systems. Experts will dive into the benefits of instant payments, including:

  • Faster cash flow management for businesses
  • Increased transaction transparency and security
  • Improved customer experience through seamless payment processing

With the world becoming more interconnected and digital-first, businesses can no longer afford to wait days for payments to clear. The discussions at SAP Inside Track Porto 2025 will provide attendees with the latest best practices for implementing instant payments into their enterprise systems, helping organizations stay competitive in a fast-paced, real-time economy.

  • Green Ledger: Sustainability as a Business Imperative

As environmental concerns grow, sustainability has become a top priority for organizations worldwide. Businesses are under increasing pressure to reduce their carbon footprints, optimize resource usage, and contribute positively to society. SAP is leading the charge in integrating sustainability into its software solutions, and Green Ledger is one of the most powerful tools in this effort.

SAP’s Green Ledger enables businesses to track, measure, and report their environmental impact, providing a comprehensive view of energy consumption, carbon emissions, and other key sustainability metrics.

Through the use of SAP solutions like SAP Cloud for Sustainable Enterprises, companies can access the data and insights they need to make more sustainable decisions across the entire supply chain. At the event, attendees will learn how to leverage SAP’s sustainability capabilities to:

  • Monitor and reduce carbon emissions
  • Track supply chain sustainability
  • Report ESG metrics to stakeholders in line with international standards

As global regulations around sustainability and environmental impact tighten, organizations need reliable tools to ensure they’re meeting compliance requirements. SAP Inside Track Porto 2025 will equip businesses with the knowledge and solutions they need to integrate sustainability into their core operations.

  • Networking and Collaboration: The Power of the SAP Community

SAP Inside Track events are not just about learning from experts – they’re about connecting with the global SAP community. SAP Inside Track Porto 2025 will bring together SAP professionals from all industries and regions, creating opportunities to share ideas, discuss challenges, and build lasting relationships. Whether you're an SAP developer, consultant, or business user, the event will offer a platform to engage with like-minded individuals and expand your professional network.

Collaborating with peers will be a central theme of the event. Attendees will have the chance to join hands-on workshops, participate in live demos, and ask questions during panel discussions. The event will also feature use case presentations, where real-world organizations share how they’ve successfully implemented SAP innovations to drive business transformation.

Conclusion: The Future is Now

SAP Inside Track Porto 2025 is not just an event; it’s a unique opportunity to dive deep into the most exciting innovations that are shaping the future of enterprise technology. From S/4HANA migration and Generative AI to Instant Payments and Green Ledger solutions, the event will provide invaluable insights into how SAP is helping businesses transform, optimize, and thrive in the digital era.

Whether you’re looking to accelerate your digital transformation, explore new technologies, or network with SAP experts and peers, SAP Inside Track Porto 2025 is the place to be. Don’t miss out on this incredible opportunity to be at the forefront of SAP innovation!

Mark your calendars – September 27th, 2025 – The Lodge Hotel Porto. We’ll see you there!

entry.title: Re: SAP IBP Planner workspace, workbook variant filtering
entry.content:
Hello, as per checking IBP roadmap, this is due until Q4 of next year.

Good luck!

Regards,

Rodrigo Jr.

entry.title: Re: Mass action in compensation is not working as expected
entry.content: Yes it is expected behavior, we will work on adding this to the documentation on the implementation guide and on the KBAs related to mass action like this one here : https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2471632

entry.title: Re: Unable to Install Replication Server . During Installation at Secondary Server mention error.
entry.content: Question: Did you find the solution? I know it's been a long time but I'm in the same boat.

entry.title: Re: Career Guidance!
entry.content:
Hi Goku,

With your background and interests, I would like to suggest pursuing a career as a functional expert. Becoming a functional expert can provide you with ample opportunities, whether as a business expert using SAP to support work in an SAP customer, as an SAP consultant, or potentially as a freelancer. The key is to truly understand business processes and how SAP technology enables them.

I believe getting into SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition will be quicker since implementations are done using a fit-to-standard approach, with most business processes clearly defined and documented. I recommend you look at the following learning resource: https://help.sap.com/learning-journeys/9b67afc133f443f9a6a08bd25d08ac59

If you want to go more into the area of functional consulting, I suggest learning some of the fundamentals: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/implementing-sap-s-4-hana-cloud-public-edition

Please take this as a recommendation for consideration from someone who has spent many years in this field. I hope this helps!

Marco

entry.title: Re: GLM / WWI grahics as vector vs. pixel
entry.content:
Hello

if SAP set up "ok" you can use whatever would like (according to my knowledge).

The "Graphic" MUST be located on any localt client and MUST be located on any generation server.

Whatever "WinWord can consume.. is from my point if view allowed

There is some "BUT".., if the phrase is used in the "raw report" (and therefore the graphic as well): the raw report might get "huge" (depening on type of extension (bmp, jpg etc.) as then the graphic is part of the raw report.

I believe: in the past there was a similar question.. could onyl find this one (not directly related).

https://community.sap.com/t5/product-lifecycle-management-q-a/insert-graphic-in-wwi-report-dynamically/qaq-p/12115695

https://community.sap.com/t5/product-lifecycle-management-q-a/wwi-insert-graphics/qaq-p/9590969

https://community.sap.com/t5/product-lifecycle-management-q-a/wwi-error-graphic-file-has-invalid-format/qaq-p/10344283

https://community.sap.com/t5/product-lifecycle-management-q-a/how-to-out-put-graphics-on-pre-printed-diamonds-rhombus-in-glm-labels/qaq-p/575329

THIS is the best i found: https://community.sap.com/t5/product-lifecycle-management-q-a/does-wwi-supports-tiff-or-png-extension-of-pictogram-file/qaq-p/10526466

https://community.sap.com/t5/product-lifecycle-management-q-a/method-for-reading-graphic-from-phrase/qaq-p/12246382

https://community.sap.com/t5/product-lifecycle-management-q-a/file-format-for-high-resolution-grafics-in-wwi/qaq-p/12345043

https://community.sap.com/t5/product-lifecycle-management-q-a/change-switch-graphic-in-transaction-cg42-in-user-exit/qaq-p/9372563

With best wishes

C.B.

How can i enable Calander in Story Filters, i kept trying, but unable to do so? Could you please help me.

If i can enable Calander instead CurrentTimeInterval, which is static here, then i can achieve my requirement.

Thank You.

entry.title: Re: Restful ABAP - RAP Abstract Entity with readonly Parameters

entry.title: Total Value in POR is converted incorrectly
entry.content:
Hi,


We created a POR in production system with currency GBP. Currency rate is maintained for GBP to EUR. However, the total value calculated by the system is not correct, it is multiplied by 10. I tried to check the configuration but I don't see issues with unit of measure since we have used the UOM YR in the past. I think it is related to the currency GBP.


Can you help to check please?


Thank you. 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Total Value in POR is converted incorrectly
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

You can refer to the below community link;

Total value currency in PR header is different with item.

Thank you

Aishwarya

entry.title: Re: Changing field value for "Assigned To" in Work Order...
entry.content: Really appreciate the response, I will continue to work on this as you suggested. And will update here this as an acceptable solution.

entry.title: Re: Error in the configuration of Hire template
entry.content: Hi there, I am receiving this same issue, and have not been able to resolve this. For me, I see this error when restarting an onboarding process after PDC has already been completed; when the onboardee tries to submit PDC again, they get this error. If the object is non effectively-dated, you can try deleting the original record as a workaround, this has worked for us in testing. Hoping someone has identified the cause of the issue, not sure why this is happening for these custom objects and not others, like Payment Info. Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Story Calculations in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content: Hi William, How can i make the Period(with Calendar) available on the top of the Story, as you did in the above reply. So my user can select particular year. For Story Filters/Input Control, i can only give my available Dimensions.

entry.title: Re: SAP CPI: JDBC On-premise database connectivity error
entry.content:
Hi Naveen,

I’m encountering the same error when trying to connect to the on-premise database from the IS. Could you please share the resolution steps if you’ve found any solutions?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: SAP Personas , script OnEnter with unwanted "enter" f...
entry.content:
Hi Emmanuel.  

I am able to replicate the issue you are having. HUPAST is such a special transaction and especially the quantity field it seems. I think it has special programming behind it.

I'm not quite sure what you are doing with the custom field and how the scanning of the EAN to the custom field translates to the value being populated into the material/handling unit field?  What I can tell you is I think I'm doing something similar in my HUPAST flavor (so that users are not typing in material numbers) and when I change my script to include setting the quantity to "1" it is not automatically packing the box.

Here is what I have done.

- users select a material line to be packed from the table at the bottom

- script button is clicked that reads the material number from the selected row and puts that material into the "material/handling unit" field

- presses enter to open the quantity field

- sets focus to a custom batch field for lot number verification (we use a barcode scanner here)

entry.title: Re: Looking for best practices on restricting order chang...
entry.content:
Hello @BarryGilbert 

You can implement own validation logic of production order changes with the BD_MFGORDER_CHECK_BEFORE_SAVE BAdI enhancement. 

In my opinion it is cleaner than modifying SAP standard status configurations.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: sh: 1: fiori: not found-> The most popular error for Fiori Developers
entry.content:
@RaminS : Make sure, you should do a multi targeted build. And you should clarify the difference between a Multi Targeted Fiori(Ui5) Application and a simple Fiori application.

  • Ensure Proper Configuration of Destination Services and HTML5 Services: Make sure to follow the steps for creating Destination Services and HTML5 services in your application as per the provided guidelines.
  • Perform .MTAR Build: Right-click on the mta.yaml file and execute a .MTAR build.
  • Discovering Generated .MTAR File: Following the .MTA.YAML build, the generated .mtar file may have a generic name such as abc_appname.mtar.
  • Deploy .MTAR File: Right-click on the generated .mtar file and select "Deploy MTAR."

    Also for your reference: $npm init, actually creates a sample package.json for your file. You need to mention the required dependencies in it.

entry.title: Re: sh: 1: fiori: not found-> The most popular error for Fiori Developers
entry.content: @RaminS  cf push will not work for these cases. It goes the mtar build and then deploy mta.yaml route.

entry.title: Re: Error in Adaptation Project with reuse component
entry.content: how does your ui5.yaml look like?

entry.title: Re: sh: 1: fiori: not found-> The most popular error for Fiori Developers
entry.content:
Well those were generic steps, but yeah for those who don't understand it:

Create a mta.yaml
then build the mta.yaml,
post build it will create a directory- "mta archives"-
inside it, you will find a file named something like "<app-name>.mtar" .
Right click on it, and then, you can deploy mta archive. 

And later on, you can find your app inside "HTML5 apps" section of your SAP BTP.

entry.title: Re: SAP IBP Planner workspace, workbook variant filtering
entry.content: Great, sounds good! Thanks a lot Rodrigo.

entry.title: Re: Looking for best practices on restricting order chang...
entry.content: Thanks, Dominik. I don't think this would be clean core, though. My project is very strict about clean core.

entry.title: Re: Intercompany credit memo
entry.content:
Hi ,

Thanks for your reply.

Please find the below screenshot shot.

1. When I tried with only Credit memo request, system is showing this error 

2. When I am selecting Billing Type IG manually then system is allowing to create IG billing document type, as copy control CR to IG 013 routine is maintained as per standard in item category level (G2N).

3. Even after creating the manual IG billing document type, system is not taking correct payer and sales area. All the intercompany billing configuration is maintained.

entry.title: Seamless Planning - Beta Program Highlights
entry.content:
Since announcing the Controlled Release of seamless planning between SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud, we’ve seen a high level of interest from our community and stakeholders, with many reaching out to learn more about available capabilities, use cases, and business impact. What better way to address these inquiries than by sharing the valuable insights and feedback we’ve gained from our customers and partners who actively tested seamless planning for the past few months through the seamless planning beta program?

Beta Setup: by using the seamless planning functionality, participants could store the SAP Analytics Cloud model’s data (fact and master data) directly in SAP Datasphere, reducing the data footprint while making it available for additional processing and extensibility scenarios through SAP Datasphere capabilities. This blog covers two of the most tested use cases during the beta program and the identified business benefits.

Use Cases

1. Creating Alignment Between Planning and Transactional Data

Business Outcome: increased agility, reduced time to action.

In many scenarios, planning data needs to be combined with transactional data from other systems, such as actuals, to create a comprehensive view for decision-making. For example, monitoring the comparison between actuals and planned data in close to real-time is essential in industries that see a rapid change in demand and market conditions. Imagine a successful marketing campaign where given products sell much faster than anticipated. In these cases, near-real-time tracking of actuals versus planned data enables businesses to identify trends and accelerate the replanning cycle. This way, operations can respond swiftly to changing market conditions – such as reallocating products across stores and distribution centers.

With seamless planning functionality, planning data can be combined with actual data immediately, allowing variance analysis and close monitoring without moving data across platforms. This real-time insight supports agile decision-making, helping businesses stay competitive while achieving their operational and financial goals. Another benefit of using SAP Datasphere as a data foundation is its ability to ensure that data across systems is ready and accurate for analysis. Combining transactional data with planned data often requires data preparation steps, such as cleaning, enhancing, transforming, and aggregating. SAP Datasphere provides key capabilities to optimize and govern these flows, bringing critical insights to users’ fingertips.

Most beta program participants tested this scenario by combining plan data with transactional data from various SAP or non-SAP sources, or by creating reports on joined data from multiple SAP Analytics Cloud models.

How it works: The SAP Analytics Cloud model data is exposed into SAP Datasphere as a table with the semantic type "fact". Using SAP Datasphere capabilities such as Graphical or SQL Views, SAP Analytics Cloud data can be combined with other data sources using standard union or join types. The final data can be consumed live in SAP Analytics Cloud stories through the SAP Datasphere’s Analytic Model.

2. Extending Calculation Logic using SQL

Business Outcome: increased flexibility in analysis and modeling.

SAP Analytics Cloud models are often enhanced with additional data and dimensions to support complex calculation logic, some of which are essential for reporting. While SAP Analytics Cloud’s advanced formula steps in data actions offer a powerful, governed environment for creating these calculations, certain use cases could benefit from the flexibility of custom code in languages like SQL or Python, allowing for even more tailored and sophisticated logic.

During the beta program, many participants tested this scenario of creating calculation logic in SAP Datasphere using SQL, a widely known and versatile language. Implementing calculations such as KPIs and metrics needed for period-end reporting in SAP Datasphere streamlined their SAC models for planning activities and reduced data integration requirements, as data required for calculations was already available in SAP Datasphere.

How it works: The SAP Analytics Cloud model data is exposed in the SAP Datasphere as a table with the semantic type "fact", allowing it to be consumed as a data source in SAP Datasphere Views. Whenever SAP Analytics Cloud end-users publish new data, the updated records are immediately available in the SAP Datasphere where calculations are performed. Once the calculations are complete, the results are instantly available in SAP Analytics Cloud through the Analytic Model.

Feedback

The positive feedback received at the end of the beta program reaffirmed that the strategic direction of closely integrating SAP Analytics Cloud with SAP Datasphere is the right approach. Key takeaways include:

  • Unanimous Value Recognition: all participants agreed that seamless planning could add significant business value to their organization.
  • Functionality Readiness: Two-thirds of participants indicated that the functionality tested in the beta program meets their current business requirements, while the remaining third expressed the need for additional features. We carefully reviewed all feature requests and were pleased to find that many align well with our planned future releases.
  • Adoption Interest: Over half of the participants are eager to adopt seamless planning within 12 months of its general availability.

Conclusion

The initial release of seamless planning lays the foundation for a future architecture that will gradually accrue more value for our customers. As we expand and enhance this functionality, customers can expect deeper integration capabilities that will streamline business operations and performance.

 

Last Call to join the seamless planning controlled release!

Are you excited about seamless planning and cannot wait until it gets released with SAP Analytics Cloud QRC1? You still have a chance to participate in the Controlled Release with SAP Analytics Cloud QRC4. 

Register here!

entry.title: Re: SAC Excel Exclude Option
entry.content:
@russellg 

Unfortunately there is no option to mass exclude the selected members. Once you paste, you have to set Exclude for each of them one by one.

 

Hope this helps 

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: Invalid char # replacement
entry.content: Do you even know which accentuated character you have? Which code page is expected in the zip file, known or unknown? You can make it work for one test file, but what about the files which will be made available in production.

entry.title: Local RAP business event consumption in S/4HANA 2023 OP
entry.content:
Hi,

I am trying to check if we can consume the business events from the accelerator hub https://api.sap.com/event/OP_PRODUCTEVENTS/overview.

I noticed that in the base BO for Product. The released RAP BO interface is actually I_PRODUCTTP_2 but usually we declare the events in the base business object which is not released for cloud development.

Now, when I tried to consume the event by creating the event class handler, I am having some issues. When I tried to use the based BO (R_PRODUCT***), I am getting an error that usage is not permitted probably because it is not released.

But if I use the released RAP BO interface I_PRODUCTTP_2, I am getting this error indicating that the released RAP BO does not define events.

Now I have few questions:

1. Are these events (ProductCreated and ProductChanged) being raised in the Manage Product Master fiori app?

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F1602')/S27OP

2. How can we consume the event using the released RAP BO? 

3. If these events can only be consumed using the SAP event mesh, can I set the S/4HANA system as both the producer and consumer of the event? More of like a round trip to the same system? Instead of having a separate BTP ABAP system to consume the event.

entry.title: Passing Bas64 Encoded string of a PDF as Email attachment from Process Automation
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I'm working on CAPM with SAP Process Automation for using workflows and sending emails. I have used AWS S3 Bucket using Object store to store my PDF attachments in CAPM. I have passed the stored file's URL  to the workflow and have created an Action in SAP BPA to fetch the content from S3 into BPA. I am able to get the Base64 Content, filename and and mime Type of the file. I have to pass this as an attachment. I checked for converting this to PDF and couldn't find a solution. I have enabled the DMS integration option which gives me the "Document Folder" type in BPA. I don't have any file path to pass and also I don't want to do a DMS or store in my local folder because my file is already saved in S3. Is there any way I can achieve this? Did I miss any tools for the same? 

SAP Build Process Automation 
@maheshpalavalli 
@chaitanya priya puvvada
@Archana
@Dan_Wroblewski

entry.title: Re: Correct way to provision the destination for calling...
entry.content:
Hello,

The new issue seems to an issue with authorization. Can you please check the destination configured 

Name: aicore-destination
Description: SAP AI Core deployed service (generative AI hub)
URL: <AI-API-INSIDE-AI-CORE-SERVICE-KEY>
Type: HTTP
ProxyType: Internet
Authentication: OAuth2ClientCredentials
tokenServiceURL: <TOKEN-SERVICE-URL-OF-AI-CORE-SERVICE-KEY>/oauth/token
clientId: <YOUR-CLIENT-ID-OF-AI-CORE-SERVICE-KEY>
clientSecret: <YOUR-CLIENT-SECRET-OF-AI-CORE-SERVICE-KEY>
# Additional Properties:
URL.headers.AI-Resource-Group: default # adjust if necessary
URL.headers.Content-Type: application/json
HTML5.DynamicDestination: true

Please replace the above parameters using the ai core service key. Also please make sure that the appropriate roles are assigned on BTP (ai core specific roles).

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Cash Management and Liquidity Planning: An Infographic
entry.content:
Is there a way to adjust the reliability of the inbound receivables. 

For example, if I am expecting $1M next week from my customer invoices based on the Due Dates, for the purpose of Cash Forecasting, can I set the S4 system to apply a blanket discount of say 20% to this receivable?

entry.title: How to find Cloud ALM global/subaccount cockpit URL from Cloud ALM direct URL
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have access to Cloud ALM direct URL only. 

https://<subdomain>.alm.cloud.sap

But I don't have information of Cloud ALM sub-account direct cockpit URL and which region it was provisioned. I don't find it from SAP ME quick launch as well.

I tried to use all the regions. It says does not exist.

https://emea.cockpit.btp.cloud.sap

https://amer.cockpit.btp.cloud.sap

https://apac.cockpit.btp.cloud.sap

Now , please tell me

How to connect to Cloud ALM global/subaccount BTP cockpit URL from Cloud ALM direct URL.

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: Error Encountered During Status change for Standard C...
entry.content:
Hi @Vera_Jiang ,

I respectfully disagree. There is no reason you or anyone else  should find it necessary to mark someone else's answer as correct, after only 7 days. When there are thousands of questions left unanswered for weeks, months, even years.

I can give you many examples of responses from product experts that don't quite answer the OP's specific question. If every answer to every question were to be marked as Correct after 7 days, this community would become quite unreliable very quickly. I've seen development communities where people vote for answers, or the OP marks an answer as correct. Never seen a product expert pick random questions and decide to mark them as answered.

Just MHO.

Isabelle

entry.title: BADI to update partner profit center while creating journal Entries

entry.title: SAP WM Annual Physical Inventory creating only 1 PI doc with 800+ quants
entry.content:
Hi - I am using tcode LI01N (WM - not IM) to create a annual physical inventory (inventory method "ST") - I want to count one mixed use bin and am getting one pi doc (with 800+ quants)

In configuration we have  - Logistics Execution> Warehouse Management> Activities> Physical Inventory> Define Differences and Document Limits ...document items set to 100 (field MINPS). 

Anyone know what triggers LI01N to create multiple PI docs per MINPS like SAP help seems to indicate it will?

Janet

entry.title: BYD2408 Schnittstelle SAP

We are planning to integrate data from the SAP system you host into our data warehouse. 
We are looking for a relatively simple solution that doesn’t require high performance but allows us to access and extract the necessary data.(for example via a REST API)
Could you please let us know what configuration is required on your end to enable data integration, including how authentication would work, 
what options are available for accessing the data, and any guidelines or limitations we should be aware of?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: BYD2408 Schnittstelle SAP
entry.content:
Hello Daniela!

In terms of accessing ByD data from external systems, we can suggest you to either use the SOAP or Odata APIs which are built into the system.

You may refer to the following documentation pages to get started on the setup. 

Odata Guide: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESS_BYDESIGN/7c182c462ec043cba338a30b952068c7/2bccd772722d1014b742a3a0c4b116d0.html?locale=en-US

SOAP Communication Scenarios: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESS_BYDESIGN/2754875d2d2a403f95e58a41a9c7d6de/2dd444ce722d1014b4ba82cecef7eb77.html?locale=en-US

Kind regards,
Leonardo
SAP Support
Community:
SAP Business ByDesign: https://www.sap.com/community/topics/business-bydesign.html
SAP Cloud for Customer: https://www.sap.com/community/topics/cloud-for-customer.html

entry.title: Re: Analytics not available for deployed APIs in SAP APIM portal
entry.content:
Hello
In my first try i had the same result, but SAP deleted my Integration Suite Trial and i begin one again
this time i activated the following the capabilities:
Build Integration Scenarios
Manage APIs
Implement Interfaces and Mappings
Manage Trading Partners
Access data in SAP Business Suite
Extend Non-SAP Connectivity

And my user have assigned this roles:
PI_Administrator
PI_Business_Expert
PI_Integration_Developer
APIPortal.Administrator
APIManagement.SelfService.Administrator

With this when i try out my api proxy in the monitor (Integration Suite Overview > Analyzes) can  see my executions.

I hope this help you.

entry.title: Re: The 'or' operation is not working across different pr...
entry.content: That's normal, I think. standard implementation expect "and" between different attributes during search.

entry.title: IBP TS Forecast consumption at Prod Cust level
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

TS Forecast consumption allows forecast consumption at PRDID , LOCID and CUSTID level.

In our scenario, we have consensus unconstrained demand at PRDID and CUSTID level. Is it possible to do forecast consumption at this level? Do we need to maintain Fcst consumption mode in any other master data type other than customer source?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: ABAP Trace to PlantUML Sequence Diagram
entry.content:
The logic to call the functionality was deactivated, but you can activate it again by

- using an implicit enhancement to reactivate the commented code in

Include LATRA_TOOL_SE30_MAINF01

FORM own_ok_code_handling 

Add this logic at the end of the FORM as an implicit enhancement:

CASE p_ok_code.
    WHEN 'OWN_UML.
      CL_ATRA_UML_HANDLING=>SHOW_SEQ_DIAGRAM( key = ls_satr_tab_key ).
 ENDCASE.

 

- You call now use the OK code OWN_UML to call the logic. 

Remember the pre-requisite: aggregation must be disabled in your SAT variant.

best regards,

Jacques

entry.title: Workflow for a Purchase order with different plants in the purchase order items
entry.content:
Hello All,

We have the below scenario for a Public cloud implementation 'Workflow for Purchase Order'. I have created a workflow that looks like this with Start conditions & Step conditions as shown below.

 

Scenario 1) For the PO with Plant I400 & Purchasing group ID0 , the approver is XYZ.

Scenario 2) For the PO multiple line items, for ex. with Plant I400 & I403 and Purchasing group ID0, the approver is ABC.

The problem is that in both the cases L1 : Local Accountant & L1: Central finance steps get triggered where the requirement is that if the PO has 1 line item with the Plant I400 then the approver should be L1:Local accountant

whereas

If the the PO has 2 line item with the Plant I400& I 403,  then the approver should be L1:Central finance.

Is there a way to handle the scenario so that system is able to determine different approvers based on Purchase order having a single plant or multiple plants in the Purchase order ?

Kindly suggest as the issue is critical.   SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Sourcing and Procurement  @JL4 @JurgenY

entry.title: Is Running SAP Commerce Cloud CCV2 on SAP Hana Database in Roadmap or pipeline in future?
entry.content: Currently SAP Commerce Cloud CCV2 only runs on Azure SQL DB in CCV2. Is Running SAP Commerce Cloud CCV2 on SAP Hana Database in Roadmap or pipeline in future?

entry.title: Re: Two Major News with SUM 2.0 SP 17
entry.content:
Hi Boris,

 

We are following DMOVE2S4 strategy for converting our customer ECC system (running on Azure) to S4HANA (RISE on GCP). We have installed additional app server on one VM (GCP) pointing to ECC system (running o Azure) and HANA DB on another VM (GCP). 

We started SUM 2.0 SP 21 PL1 on additional app server (GCP) of ECC (Azure). We selected Expert Mode option, deselected Activate DMO with System Move. It proceeded with select roadmap phase after giving Stack File. During the extraction roadmap, it scanned all media files and at the end it checks for ASCS instance which it is not getting. We have tried solutions from SAP note 3324764, 3120148 but it did not help. 

I tried to install ASCS instance in the additional app server VM (GCP) for same ECC (Azure) system but it did not work which was expected. Now question is, SAP ECC additional app server knows that it has ASCS instance but SUM is not able to detect the same. I am not able to figure out where is the GAP ?? Is this scenario supported for DMOVE2S4 or not ?  Your help is much appreciated. 

 

Best Regards,

Sandeep Jain

entry.title: Restricting visibility of Pay Components in "Add New Employee" page
entry.content:
Hi Community,

 

In the "Add New Employee" page, both recurring and non-recurring pay components are appearing in the recurring pay components dropdown under the Compensation Information section. This might be causing confusion for users when selecting the appropriate pay component type.

 

Is there any way so that non-recurring pay components will get displayed separately from recurring ones?

 

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

entry.title: CJ88 unable to settle revenue correctly
entry.content:
There is a project  with  revenue affecting net income  5000  in period 10. when I executed analysis result in kka2，but not posting in cj88

 the revenue affecting net income is shown 6000  in period 11,Is it possible that  revenue posting   get periodic amount instead of cumulative amout from result analysis   when I used cj88

entry.title: Re: Key Highlights from the 2H 2024 Release of SuccessFactors Onboarding
entry.content: So they pulled the people profile changes???

entry.title: Re: CJ88 unable to settle revenue correctly
entry.content:
In SAP ERP, the issue you're facing with CJ88 not posting revenue correctly could be linked to the way results analysis (RA) is configured, particularly how periodic amounts are handled.

  1. Periodic vs. Cumulative Revenue Posting: In CJ88, when you execute settlement for a project, the system usually settles the cumulative results analysis amounts, which reflect all revenue postings up to the current period. However, if the system is posting the amount for only the current period, it might be due to the configuration in the RA or settlement rules.

    • Check the Results Analysis (RA) Method used in the project. Ensure that it is configured to post the correct cumulative or periodic amounts. Some RA methods may be set to calculate on a cumulative basis, while others may use periodic amounts, depending on how the settlement profiles and the result analysis strategy are set up.

  2. Settlement Rule Configuration: Go to the project settlement rule (Transaction: CJ02) and check the configuration for the settlement type. If it’s set to "Revenue" for settlement, verify that the settlement parameters (such as period selection) align with what you're expecting for the revenue amounts.

  3. RA/Settlement Period Selection: Check the result analysis and settlement period settings. If the system is considering the wrong period for posting, it could cause discrepancies between what is shown in KKA2 and what gets posted in CJ88. You may need to check if there is a mismatch between the revenue in period 10 and the system’s RA calculation, possibly influenced by the "periodic" setting.

  4. RA Line Items Review: Use transaction KKA2 to analyze the result items. You might need to check if there is a variance between the cumulative and periodic amounts that the system is considering for the settlement. This could indicate that the calculation is not matching the expected settlement structure.

  5. Check for Posting Logs: If you have configured posting control in the project system or revenue postings, check if there are any errors or warnings related to the settlement in the logs of transaction CJ88. There may be a restriction or configuration causing only the periodic amount to be posted, instead of the cumulative amount.

To summarize:

  • Verify the RA method and settings to ensure it is not set to only post periodic amounts.
  • Check the settlement rule and configuration to ensure the correct amounts are being used in the settlement transaction.
  • Inspect the KKA2 analysis for periodic and cumulative data to confirm that the amounts match the expectations for the settlement.

entry.title: Modification of PO Field
entry.content:
Good day,

I am currently part of a Finance Transformation team, and my client is a company that has Purchase Orders that have more symbols than want we have as a maximum in SAP. Could you please let me know, how I can modify the Purchase Order field in SAP to have up to 20-25 characters?


Thank you in advance for your help,
Nikola Nikolov

entry.title: Re: Unicode Conversion Issue - ABAP, modification in database table.
entry.content:
Hi Rangan,

for MODIFY, your internal table should correspond to the database table structure. So try to select from ztfre_layout with " * " instead of "min_stock, zvalto"

In this case, an UPDATE on ztfre_layout with "SET min_stock = 0" should work too, without selecting the data beforehand. Check the documentation here.

entry.title: Re: Complete Table and Method to attain Table Number for...
entry.content:
In SAP ERP, SE16N (or SE16) is a transaction used for table display and data exploration. While there isn't a single predefined "complete list" of tables available for SE16N, you can access the relevant tables for a specific transaction code (T-code) or business process in a few ways. Here's how to look up tables for any particular T-code, such as VK11 (Condition Records):

Finding Tables for a Specific T-code (e.g., VK11 for Condition Records):

  1. Use the Technical Information (F1 Help):

    • Navigate to the transaction or screen you're interested in, such as VK11.
    • Once in the transaction, press F1 to get the field help for the relevant field or screen.
    • Click on the Technical Information button (or icon). This will display the underlying table names and field names for the current screen or field.
    • The Table/View field in the Technical Information window will give you the name of the table(s) used for that specific field or screen.

  2. Using Transaction SE11 (ABAP Dictionary):

    • You can also search for the related tables using SE11, the ABAP Dictionary transaction.
    • In SE11, you can search for Database Tables.
      • Enter the relevant name or the part of the name (like KONV for Condition Records) to identify the tables used in the process.
      • For condition records (VK11), the main table is KONV, which stores condition records, and KONP, which stores the condition values.

  3. Using the "Where-Used List" in SE11:

    • If you know a specific object or table (e.g., KONV for Condition Records), you can search for its Where-Used List.
    • In SE11, go to Where-Used List and enter the table name, such as KONV.
    • This will show you all the places where this table is used, including the T-codes or programs that interact with it.

  4. Explore the Transaction Code via the Menu Path:

    • Each transaction code (T-code) corresponds to certain programs, function modules, and tables.
    • In some cases, you can find the relevant tables used for a T-code by reviewing the menu path and technical objects in SE93 (Customizing for Tcodes) or ST03N (Workload Analysis).

Example for VK11 (Condition Records):

  • KONV: This is the main table for condition records in SAP, which contains condition values.
  • KONP: Contains condition records for pricing.
  • AUSP: Stores attributes for condition records.
  • You can also explore views like V_002 (View for pricing conditions) or check tables related to pricing procedures in T685 for condition types and related pricing information.

Using SE16N Directly:

  • In SE16N, you can enter the table name directly (for example, KONV, KONP, or AUSP) to explore the data.
  • If you want to see related tables, look for foreign key relationships in SE11 (ABAP Dictionary) or technical information from the field help.

General Steps for Finding Tables in SE16N:

  1. Open SE16N.
  2. Enter a known table name (e.g., KONV for condition records).
  3. You can also use Table joins or explore views for more complex scenarios.

Summary:

  1. F1 Help > Technical Info for a specific field in any T-code.
  2. Use SE11 to search for relevant tables related to a T-code or business process.
  3. Use Where-Used List to find all places where a table is referenced.
  4. Check Table/Field Relationships and Views for comprehensive data access.

By following these steps, you can find the relevant tables for any T-code, like VK11 for condition records, and then explore them further in SE16N.

entry.title: Re: Workflow for a Purchase order with different plants i...
entry.content:
To handle this scenario in SAP Public Cloud for a Purchase Order (PO) workflow where the approver depends on whether the PO contains a single plant or multiple plants, 

Steps to Implement:

  1. Define Workflow Conditions Based on Plant Count:

    • You need to define a condition that checks whether the PO contains a single plant (i.e., one plant in the line items) or multiple plants. This can be achieved by creating a condition based on the number of unique plants in the PO line items.

  2. Customizing the Workflow:

    • Use Custom Conditions in Workflow: You can leverage custom conditions in the workflow to count the distinct plants in the PO. For example:
      • In the workflow step condition, define a custom function or rule to count the distinct plants in the PO lines.
      • If the count of distinct plants is 1 (only I400), then set the approver as L1: Local Accountant.
      • If the count of distinct plants is greater than 1 (I400 & I403), set the approver as L1: Central Finance.

  3. Create Custom Business Rules:

    • In SAP Public Cloud, Business Rules Framework (BRF+) can be used to define custom logic. You could create a business rule to evaluate the line items of the PO and determine the number of unique plants.
    • Once the number of unique plants is identified, assign the appropriate workflow step.

  4. Defining Approver Assignment:

    • In your workflow, define a step where the approver is assigned based on the rule you created above:
      • For Scenario 1 (single plant), assign approver XYZ as L1: Local Accountant.
      • For Scenario 2 (multiple plants), assign approver ABC as L1: Central Finance.

Example of Logic:

  1. Step Condition:

    • Create a custom step condition that evaluates the PO line items. For example, you can use a script or a BRF+ rule to count the number of distinct plants in the PO items.

  2. Branching Logic:

    • If the number of unique plants equals 1, assign approver XYZ (Local Accountant).
    • If the number of unique plants is greater than 1, assign approver ABC (Central Finance).

Configuring in SAP Public Cloud:

  1. Transaction or Application in Public Cloud: You will likely use the Manage Workflows application in SAP Public Cloud to configure the workflow steps and conditions.

    • When creating the workflow, use the conditions and steps based on the above logic.
    • The workflow should trigger based on the number of unique plants present in the PO.

  2. Testing the Workflow:

    • After setting up the conditions and steps, thoroughly test the workflow with different scenarios (PO with single plant vs. multiple plants) to ensure the correct approvers are assigned.

entry.title: Re: Hello SAP Community,Time and Cost Collection for Qual...
entry.content:
Hello，

For question1: The QM order function is not available in S/4HANA Public Cloud.
For question2: Per my knowledge, there is no workaround to achieve the similar function. You may also wait for the input from others.

SAP Support
Matt

entry.title: Re: IS Retail: Customer master table KNVV is missing
entry.content:
the issue regarding missing entries in the KNVV table while processing IDOCs in SAP IS-Retail! This explanation will certainly help others who face similar challenges in the future. 

Issue:

While processing IDOCs, you encountered an error: "Customer master table KNVV is missing."

Root Cause:
The division assigned to the site (e.g., JH) was not available in the KNVV table for the respective customer, causing the IDOC processing to fail.

Solution Steps:

  1. Verify Existing Data in KNVV Table:

    • Use SE16N or SE11 to check the KNVV table for the customer and verify if the relevant Sales Area (Sales Organization, Distribution Channel, Division) exists.

  2. Check the Site’s Division:

    • Use T-Code WB03 to display the site and identify its assigned Division.

  3. Update Customer Sales Area Data:

    • Use T-Code XD02:
      • Enter the Customer Number.
      • Add the missing Sales Organization, Distribution Channel, and Division.
      • Click the Create icon to add these details.

  4. Confirm Changes in KNVV:

    • Recheck the KNVV table to ensure the new entries for the relevant Sales Area are created successfully.

  5. Reprocess IDOCs:

    • Use T-Code BD87 to reprocess the failed IDOCs.
    • Verify successful processing.

  6. Alternative T-Code for Confirmation:

    • You can also use T-Code WPER to confirm successful processing of IDOCs.

Note:

  • Ensure that the Sales Area updates align with the organizational structure and processes in SAP IS-Retail.
  • Regular checks for master data consistency can prevent such issues from recurring.

entry.title: Meaningless "posted a topic/replied to topic", instead use "question", "discussion", "blog article"
entry.content:
I see in my Notification feed questions, discussions and blog posts which are all tagged "posted a new topic" or "replied to topic", and I need to look at the picture to know if it's a question, a blog post or even a discussion. It has taken me months to change my habit from going from the text to the picture, in fact I have to look at both parts, the kind of topic, and the topic title. Why not having the whole information in the text? Proposing to replace "topic" with either "question", "discussion" or "blog article".

Here, it's a question:

It's a blog post:

It's a discussion:

Surprisingly, "topic" is not used in "commented on blog article":

Thank you.

Sandra

entry.title: Re: Workflow hierarchy approval setup for condition contract
entry.content: Is there any report or tcode where we can see approval status contract no wise?

entry.title: Budget Transfer Timing for STO Transactions
entry.content:
Hello All,

I hope this message finds you well.

I have a query regarding our current process for transferring materials between projects using Stock Transport Orders (STOs) within our multiple SAP plants. When we transfer materials from Project A to Project B, the associated budget amount is immediately debited from Project A and credited to Project B, even if the material is still in transit or the Goods Receipt Notice (GRN) is pending.

This early budget transfer complicates our financial tracking, as the funds are shifted before the materials are officially received at Plant B.

Is there a solution or configuration within SAP that would allow us to withhold the budget transfer until the materials have been received and verified at Plant B? Your insights on optimizing this process would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you for your assistance!

entry.title: New Live Session: Starting with AI in SAP Customer Experience Solutions
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community! I’m back (again) writing about some SAP CX Product Learning news!

Did you know that there a a lot of Use Cases for Artificial Intelligence in SAP Customer Experience solutions?

Some of them rely on Predictive AI like Lead Scoring in SAP Sales Cloud, Others are build on top of Generative AI like Personalized Product descriptions for SAP Commerce Cloud, Also you can use embedded AI in the solution or leverage the CX AI Toolkit to promote Conversational AI utilization based on Joule.

At first I was also a bit overwhelmed about this AI topic 😰, so get into it 🤓, after a digesting it 🐳, I have prepared 👨‍🍳 the Starting with AI in SAP Customer Experience Solutions Live Session, so that you can get in one hour quite an overview of the AI topic.

During this live session, we'll start covering briefly the SAP Business AI strategy, and the particular strategy for CX. Later, we'll cover most relevant uses cases for AI across the whole SAP Customer Experience solutions portfolio with explanations but also live demos performed on the systems. We finalize with more resources for you to continue learning about the topic.

Let’s explore together AI use cases for SAP CX Solutions!

This session will be held twice per quarter during 2025, and next edition is on December 2024. and as I’ve said, the live session is already available on SAP Learning. You will need to have a subscription in order to register for this live session there or watch the recording of a previous edition. 

For better understanding of this live session’s content, it’s recommended you know already a bit about the different SAP Customer Experience Solutions before attending to this informative live session. No previous AI knowledge is required.

Awesome! thanks for reading until the end of this post🎆! I’ll return very soon with new topics, but until then, please share any comments or suggestions below.

entry.title: Where can I configure the “To-Do Task Reminder” email template from the Manager start page?
entry.content:
On the start page under “My To-Do's” there is a “My Team” tile where manager can send reminder messages to employees who have overdue forms or overdue items. Unfortunately, the email does not contain a signature. Unfortunately, our managers do not add them either.

I cannot find this message in the LMS under “Manage Email” “Email Notification Templates” or in the BizX (we do not have Employee Central) "Email Notification Templates" to configure it.

Does anyone have a tip for me?

Thank you very much
Sandra

entry.title: Re: Issue where standard CDS View cannot be imported in D...
entry.content:
@rio_tk

Please create a new cds view with source as I_GLACCOUNT

add the annotation @Analytics.dataExtraction.enabled: true and activate the view.

The newly created view will be visible under remote table section

entry.title: DRTM Master data Purge
entry.content:
We tried purging inactive user via DRTM Master data Purge. The approver did not receive any approval in Purge Request monitor and purge progress and results - it directly gave result as "completed with empty report"
Request your support as this is on priority

Thanks
SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central

entry.title: What is aggregation factor for product groups in Sales and operation planning
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,

I am new to PP module and I want to understand what is aggregation factor that we assign to materials while creating product group in standard sales operation planning. I have executed multiple scenarios with aggregation factor. I have used aggregation factor as 0,1,2 and 5 but it didn't influence the procedure. Then how aggregation factor works and how does it affect he procedure.

Could you please give me insights ?

 

Shrikant

entry.content: Thanks for answering, I will try this

entry.title: Re: How to provide default date with cap actions
entry.content: Thanks for answering, I will try this...please let me know if you can answer this

entry.title: Re: How to provide default date with cap actions
entry.content: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/progress-indicator-in-sap-cap/qaq-p/13928235

entry.title: Re: Posting to vendor accounts are not supported in TRM
entry.content: SAP TRM Vendor subledger posting happens in one sap instance, but at another instance same configuration not happenning giving 000002 error, accoun type not assigned, where as it is assigned correctly. Counterparty Role Vendor :Payment transactions tab is not showing. Is this a reason for failing  posting to FI.

entry.title: Re: Error in Adaptation Project with reuse component
entry.content: did it work before? any error information?

entry.title: Re: Posting to vendor accounts are not supported in TRM
entry.content: Can any one help.

entry.title: Re: Posting to vendor accounts are not supported in TRM
entry.content:
It is success in another instance .

Here BP  in counterparty in Vendorpart tab is different from other instance where Vendor posting error. please see below:

This Vendor Payment Details not showing another system in BP where Vendor posting error. I have succesfully vendor posting in borrowing 200 Interest rate instrument without using BADI. Only subledger vendor, Account Key 34.

With this BP vendor Subledger not happening in another system. In other system , without BADI , Vendor subledger in S4 Hana posting happens .

Can any one know why here not happening. is due to SAP note or any config. Config perfect. Only difference here Vendor Payment details TAB not showing in this BP in counterparty in co-code data

entry.title: SAP Alerts are not sending
entry.content:
Dear SAP Experts,


     We want to send Alerts to Users and for that purpose we have done the following:
1. Enable Alerts.
2. Restarted the Messaging service.
3. Restarted Job work services.
But then too, alerts are not sent.
Please provide any resolution or suggestion on this.

 

Regards,

Gazanfar Ali Shaikh

entry.title: Re: Posting to vendor accounts are not supported in TRM
entry.content:
Other system , without BADI, Vendor subledger posting for borrowing happened.Below is the entry.

Below is Reconciliation correctly in Vendor Reconciliation GL also without using BADI in Treasury. point is what is the reason for another system not happening. Is this difference in TAB Vendor Payment TAB not appearing in another one instance. Anyone please comment

entry.title: Re: Error in Adaptation Project with reuse component
entry.content: In Production, with OData service replacement, without list report and object page enhancement; it is working. with new requirement, added custom action by extending list report controller. List report page is working fine but after navigation to object object, attachment service reuse component is trying to find "attachmentReuseComponent::simple-attachment:: attachmentContainer" and fails with error. Resulting in only object page header visible other sections and footer are not loading.

entry.title: Re: Posting to vendor accounts are not supported in TRM
entry.content:
BADI is not required for TRM Vendor subledger posting if you do correct config.

My mail id:subashmohanty25@gmail.com

entry.title: Re: Android Build Error without Log
entry.content:
Hi @Sankara1 ,

Thank you very much for your reply. I think you meant release latest 4.12.132? Since ..108 has been the latest release before Thursday.

Anyway, I get the same result; an error without any log information. Tried it several times. iOS builds are without any problems. Anything else you can do?

entry.title: Re: SAP CPI: JDBC On-premise database connectivity error
entry.content:
MS SQL is not the same as MySQL, and there is not an available MySQL driver in CPI.

 

Regards,

Ryan Crosby

entry.title: Re: Loading Time by Crane to be displayed on Order confirmation and Delivery confirmation (outbound)
entry.content: This seems like a reasonable way to approximate the time/cost calculating based on weight.  But you will need a table to define the time per ton of different materials.  To get actual time you'd need to implement an IOT or mobile scenario where actual time per delivery is either automatically or manually recorded.

entry.title: Hi Please help me with error. G/L account 4000 cannot be used (please correct)Message no. ME045
entry.content:
G/L account 4000 cannot be used (please correct)

Message no. ME045

Diagnosis

Comparison of the field selection strings from the G/L account 4000 and the account assignment category K reveals that there is an incompatible combination of field selections for the field selection group 'Cost center'.

The G/L account field selection is stored in table T004F; that for the account assignment is stored in table T162K.

Procedure

Please enter a correct G/L account.

entry.title: Unable to populate Column names dynamically for Restricted Measures in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hi All,

For my below requirement, i created 5 restricted measures on periods with range of 12*15 periods and offsets. My problem is, here i'm unable to populate the Column names, based periods restricted in measure input control. 

Here in the Restricted Measure - Calculation Editor, there is no option to create Name as Dynamic Text for Column names based on InputControl values

Could someone please help.

For Story, I'm using Classic Design Experience as Blending is supported yet in Optimized Design Experience.

entry.title: Closing Zero-Balance Time Accounts to Hide from Employee Profiles Using SAP Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Managing time accounts in SuccessFactors Time Management can sometimes require a customized approach, especially when the goal is to improve user experience by hiding time accounts with zero balances. This blog discusses a custom integration solution using SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) to close time accounts with zero balance, effectively hiding them from employees’ profiles when applying for absences.

Business Requirement

In our organization, there was a need to remove zero-balance time accounts from employee profiles to streamline the absence application process. While exploring options within SuccessFactors, we found no out-of-the-box solution to achieve this. As a result, we developed a custom integration to close these zero-balance time accounts using SAP Cloud Integration.

Key Considerations

Before implementing this solution, it’s essential to clarify the following requirements with the business stakeholders:

  1. Define Specific Time Accounts: Determine which time account types need to be closed at zero balance, so only these accounts are included in the interface configuration.

  2. Exclude Future Postings: The interface should close only those time accounts with a zero balance as of today’s date, ignoring any future-dated absences.

  3. Avoid Future-Dated Time Accounts: Exclude any time accounts with a future start date (e.g., those starting on January 1, 2025) from the interface, ensuring only currently active time accounts are included.

Solution Design: SAP Cloud Integration Flow

 

The following steps outline the integration flow (iFlow) developed to accomplish the closure of zero-balance time accounts in SuccessFactors.

Step 1: Set Properties

  • Content Modifier: Set the property “todaydate” to capture the current date.

 

Step 2: Retrieve Time Account Data

  • Request-Reply: Fetch time account data from SuccessFactors, filtering for active accounts where the booking start date is on or before today’s date.

 

Step 3: Filter Applicable Time Accounts

  • XSLT Script - Filter_Time_Accounts: This script is designed to filter only those time accounts that need to be closed when their balance is zero.

 

Step 4: Exclude Future-Dated Absences

  • XSLT Script - Exclude Future Dated Bookings: Excludes any time accounts with future-dated bookings, ensuring that only accounts with past or current dates are processed.

 

Step 5: Calculate Available Balance

  • XSLT Script - Calculate Available Balance: This script calculates the remaining balance for each time account. For example, if an employee has 16 hours and uses two 8-hour absences, the balance would reach zero (16 - 8 - 8 = 0).

 

Step 6: Filter Zero-Balance Accounts

  • XSLT Script - Filter Zero Balance: This script filters accounts with a zero balance, extracting relevant data fields like external code, user ID, and setting “accountclosed” to true.

 

Step 7: Split Records Per Time Account

  • Splitter: Splits the records by each time account to individually send closure details to SuccessFactors.

 

Step 8: Update SuccessFactors

  • Request-Reply: Posts the updated time account details back to SuccessFactors, closing each zero-balance account in the system.

 

Outcome in SuccessFactors

  • Before: Employees could see two time accounts with zero balances.

 

  • After: The integration closes only the active zero-balance time account (e.g., the current year’s account) while leaving future-dated accounts (e.g., Time Account 2025) unaffected.

 

 

This solution effectively hides zero-balance time accounts from employees, enhancing the user experience and ensuring they see only relevant information in their profiles. Through SAP Cloud Integration, organizations can better manage employee time accounts, keeping the interface clean and intuitive.

entry.title: Re: SCIM API Filter is throwing an error when using SAP SuccessFactors schema extensions
entry.content: I have opened a ticket with SAP Support and will share the result here.

entry.title: Entering WBS Code in Sales Order Type:CCFU
entry.content:
Hi Expert！

I want to enter a WBS for a item in Sales Order Type: CCFU (Consignment).
However, the WBS field is grayed out and I cannot enter anything.

Is there any solution?
 
 
Soma
